Unemployment Claims Dropped Last Week
data.description:
By Matt Grossman


U.S. jobless claims ticked lower last week, showing that despite weaker hiring, the labor market has continued to avoid a rise in layoffs.

About 198,000 Americans filed new claims for unemployment benefits in the week through Jan. 10, versus 207,000 a week earlier, according to figures released by the Labor Department on Thursday morning. Economists polled by The Wall Street Journal were expecting to see 215,000 claims this past week.

Continuing claims, a gauge of the size of the total unemployed population, fell to 1.88 million in the week through Jan. 3, from 1.9 million the week before. The continuing-claims data lag the initial-claims numbers by a week.

Hiring has cooled over the past 12 months, yet the weekly jobless-claims data have provided consistent evidence that layoffs have remained restrained. That has put the labor market into a sometimes uncomfortable balance, in which unemployment has only risen gradually and remains modest by historical standards, despite a dearth of new opportunities for workers.

The December jobs report last week showed that the economy added 50,000 new jobs last month, while unemployment ticked down to 4.4%. Hiring over October and November, however, was revised lower.


Write to Matt Grossman at matt.grossman@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

01-15-26 0855ET

Kroger Launches Fast, Convenient Delivery on Uber Apps Nationwide
data.description:
Uber Technologies, Inc. and The Kroger Co. announced the launch of nearly 2,700 Kroger Family of Companies stores on the Uber Eats, Uber, and Postmates apps. Customers nationwide can now shop their local Kroger banner for fresh groceries, Kroger's Brands favorites, household essentials, and more with the convenience of on-demand and same day delivery via Uber's apps.

This rollout delivers on the companies' previously announced plan to broaden access, choice, and value for millions of households. In addition to Kroger's floral and sushi shops already available on Uber Eats, customers can browse full store assortments from Ralphs, Fred Meyer, King Soopers, Smith's, Fry's, Harris Teeter, Mariano's, and additional Kroger banners, then schedule delivery at the time that works best for them. To mark the launch, Uber is offering customers discounts of up to 50% off their first order from a variety of Kroger banners.

Additionally, Uber One members enjoy $0 Delivery Fee++ everyday on eligible orders in addition to other benefits designed to make everyday shopping even more convenient.

New York Factory Activity Rises
data.description:
By Don Nico Forbes


New York State manufacturing activity swung back into positive territory this month following an unexpected fall in December, driven by new orders and shipments.

The Federal Reserve Bank of New York said Thursday that its statewide manufacturing index of business conditions rose eleven points to 7.7 in January, from minus 3.7 last month. A consensus of economists polled by The Wall Street Journal has anticipated a smaller climb to 1.0.

"After a small dip in December, manufacturing activity increased modestly in New York State in January," said Richard Deitz, economic research adviser at the New York Fed.

New orders increased, while the index for shipments surged to its highest level in more than a year, the New York Fed said.

The prices paid index held steady, indicating that input price rises remained elevated, though the prices received index fell to its lowest level since February 2025.

Delivery times were unchanged. Indexes for unfilled orders and supply availability both slipped.

Employment fell after rising in the two previous months, while capital spending plans ticked up.

The index for future business showed that about half of respondents expect conditions to improve over the next six months.


Write to Don Nico Forbes at don.forbes@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

01-15-26 0845ET

US weekly jobless claims unexpectedly fall amid seasonal adjustment challenges
data.description:
WASHINGTON, Jan 15 (Reuters) - The number of Americans filing new applications for unemployment benefits unexpectedly fell last week, but that likely does not signal a material shift in the labor market, which remains in a holding pattern.

Initial claims for state unemployment benefits dropped 9,000 to a seasonally adjusted 198,000 for the week ended January 10, the Labor Department said on Thursday. Economists polled by Reuters had forecast 215,000 claims for the latest week.

The surprise drop in claims likely reflected continuing challenges adjusting the data for seasonal fluctuations around the year-end holiday season and the start of the year. There has been little change in labor market dynamics, with layoffs remaining low and hiring sluggish.

Economists say President Donald Trump's aggressive trade and immigration policies have reduced both demand for and supply of workers. Businesses are also unsure of their staffing needs as they invest heavily in artificial intelligence, curbing hiring.

The Federal Reserve's Beige Book report on Wednesday said "employment was mostly unchanged" in early January. The U.S. central bank said multiple districts "reported an increase in the usage of temporary workers, with one contact reporting this allows them 'to stay flexible in uncertain times'." When firms were hiring, it was "mostly to backfill vacancies rather than create new positions," the Fed added.

The government reported last week that nonfarm payrolls increased by 50,000 jobs in December. The economy added 584,000 jobs in 2025, the fewest in five years, averaging about 49,000 positions per month. The unemployment rate fell to 4.4% from 4.5% in November. But long-term unemployment remains prevalent.

The number of people receiving unemployment benefits after an initial week of aid, a proxy for hiring, decreased 19,000 to a seasonally adjusted 1.884 million during the week ended January 3, the claims report showed.

(Reporting By Lucia Mutikani; Editing by Chizu Nomiyama and Andrea Ricci)
